qt: Which one of the following is most appropriate sequence for effective writing?
To keep a sentence effective, the suggested average sentence length is:
All of the following steps are found in the planning stage of business writing, EXCEPT:
Which of the following cannot result from incomplete messages?
_________________ is often regarded as an undesirable thing, which prevents the best ideas from being adopted.
Careful outlining of topics is helpful for all of the given reasons EXCEPT:
op: It enables to emphasize properly the various topics in the message
It improves the clarity of message
It keeps the suspense in message

qt: “His black assistant speaks more clearly than he does”. Which kind of biasness this sentence shows?
All of the following steps are found in the planning stage of business writing; Except
Which one of the following is not required to keep conciseness in writing?
Diagrams are excellent for conveying all of the following; Except
Ali has to write a research paper. He has finalized the topic and collected the initial data. Now he is going to write the first paragraph. Which stage of effecting writing it depicts?
_______________ is the language of business correspondence written for a general readership.
The direct method is adopted at organising stage of effective business writing for which of the following purposes?
op: Communicating a negative message or bad-news

qt: To find the right media for your presentation you need to first determine which one of the following?
All of the following are the characteristics of concrete writing EXCEPT:
In written communication, use of active verbs helps make sentences more:
In which stage of Group formation Members work to solve conflicts and recognize acceptable kinds of conduct?
All of the following are considered as some basic truths about human nature that help us humanize our business messages, EXCEPT:
The purpose of your message will determine:
op: How much you need to know about the background of your letter.
How much you need to know about your idea.
How much you need to know about your reader.

qt: All of the following factors bring the element of completeness in a message EXCEPT:

“His GPA in 2000(MBA) was 3.9 on a four point scale.” 
Which of the following is a correct and more concrete example of above statement?
op: His GPA was 3.9 on a four point scale.
He got a good score in his MBA Program.

qt: Diagrams are excellent for conveying all of the following, except :
Visual aids perform which of the following functions?
op: Illustrating how things related to one another

qt: Which one of the following information is true about bar charts, simple column charts and multi-column charts?
op: Can be used to illustrate varied values
Are usually not good visual aids
All can be used effectively for the same purpose
Are usually  illegal for visual aids

qt: When considering objects or models as visual aids, the presenter should consider which of the following point?
op: There are situations in which the object being discussed or a realistic model makes the best support
The object or model needs to be passed around the audience during the speaker's presentation so the audience-members can get a look at it while it is described
Objects and models are too big to use in a presentation
Audience-members can easily learn as much from other visual aides

qt: Writing with a you-attitude shows sincere ______________ for the reader.
When using visuals in a presentation, which of the following should be avoided?
op: Taking time to explain the visual.
Fitting the visual to the material being discussed.

qt: When applied to business messages 'correctness' means all of the following, EXCEPT:
op: Check accuracy of words, information and data
Use the right level of language

qt: Suppose there is a group meeting and in the end all the group members agree on the final decision through discussion and debate. What kin d on solution is it?
Which of the following method of outlining speech is generally preferred for negative news messages?
Which of the following is/are central feature(s) of a group?
Supporting material can serve which of the following functions?
Which of the following method of outlining topic involves presenting a general statement first, followed by specific supporting statements?
When do you think groups, rather than individuals working alone, should be used to solve a problem?
op: When a quick resolution is essential
When the task requires a limited amount of information and skills
When commitment to the decision is important
When the task is fairly simple and straightforward

qt: Which of the following can help the presenter to avoid a computer catastrophe?
op: Have a contingency plan and back-up technical support available

qt: Which of the following is the most appropriate definition of group communication?
op: Two or more persons who are interacting with one another during which mutual influence is taking place
Any collection of two or more persons
Any time two or more persons influence each other
Any interaction between two or more persons

qt: While preparing for effective business writing Planning Stage involves all of the following, EXCEPT:
While preparing for effective business writing Drafting Stage involves all of the following, EXCEPT:
In a well planned presentation where there is no need to record information that comes up on the spot, there may be no need for:
Which of the following are central features of a group?
All of the following are considered as the core values of correctness, except:
Which of the following is one of the major benefits of 35mm slides?
op: The slides can be easily duplicated
The duplicated 35mm slides carry different messages to different people
35mm slides can be used for large audience

qt: In an effective writing Emphasis stands out in two positions i.e. at/in:
Which of the following method of outlining topic presents the specific information first, followed by conclusion?
Which of the following is NOT among the drawbacks of using computerized design programs?
Which of the following is NOT the stage of effective business writing?
All of the following statements about groups are true, EXCEPT:
op: Group members strive to achieve some common purpose
Group members influence and are influenced by one another

qt: What is the basic requirement of Coordinating behavior?
op: It requires the exchange of messages whether verbal or nonverbal
It requires the use of nonverbal communication only
It requires the use of verbal communication only
It requires that group members be in the same geographic area

qt: While using a flip board in presentation, 6x6 formula is used. What does it refers to?
op: 6 sentences with 6 different colors on one chart
6 lines, each having 6 words on a sheet
6 words in every sentence written in 6 different colors

qt: Which of the given sentences is bias free?
op: Mr. Usman, aged 55, has just joined our Technical Department.
Please share this report with your supervisor.He will find it interesting.
Workers with physical disabilities face many barriers on the job.
Mr. Umer is an unusually tall Asian.

qt: Which one of the following should not be interpreted to mean brevity, which will result in an incomplete message?
Effective writers visualize the ___________ before starting to write.
All of the following guidelines play an important part to make your writing concrete, EXCEPT:
op: Use active rather than passive verbs.
Include as much specific information as possible.

qt: While determining the order of the topics, one should review the outline, keeping all of the following questions in mind, EXCEPT:
op: Is the sequence of the topics appropriate for the development method I am using?
Is the sequence of the topics likely to add clarity to my message?
Are the ideas of equal importance presented in a parallel manner?

qt: Select the correct option upon which the presentation through visual aid depends rather than presenter:
What can be built through positive wording in written communication?
Diversity is important to the success of groups. What does it mean?
op: Group members should encourage and support diversity
Group members should be honest in their personal views toward diversity
Co-cultures can contribute to group dysfunction if differences dominate group goals

qt: All of the following are the elements of effective written communication, EXCEPT:
In written communication active verbs help make sentences more:
Which of the following method is generally preferred when presenting positive or good-news information?
All of the following are the benefits of handouts EXCEPT:
op: Handouts can be used with all types of presentation media.
Handouts eliminate the need for note taking.
Handouts passed out in advance tend to cause attendees to prejudge the presenter and/or the presentation.
Handouts provide an accurate record of the information being presented.

qt: _________________ is often regarded as an undesirable thing. which prevents the best ideas from being adopted.
When applied to business messages 'correctness' means all of the following. EXCEPT:
op: Use the right level of language
Check accuracy of words. information and data

qt: Which one of the following should not be interpreted to mean brevity. which will result in an incomplete message?
“His GPA in 2000(MBA) was 3.9 on a four point scale.” Which of the following is a correct and more concrete example of above statement?
op: His GPA was 3.9 on a four point scale.
He got a good score in his MBA Program.
